paper_title: Experimental Set-up and Study Species for HP Interference Testing

methods.description: This method involves assessing the average number of pollen grains per anther for each species to prepare pollen mixtures for HP interference testing.

steps.description: Collect a fixed number of anthers and extract the ripe pollen grains by shaking them in a Coulter-Isoton-IIdiluent-buffer solution.
steps.searchable_summary: collect anthers and extract pollen grains using a buffer solution

steps.description: Count the number of pollen grains with a Coulter Counter Multisizer.
steps.searchable_summary: count pollen grains using Coulter Counter Multisizer
outputs: average number of pollen grains per anther

methods.description: This method describes the preparation of pollen mixtures and their application to test for HP interference by manipulating HP and CP deposition.

steps.description: Prepare pollen mixtures by combining an approximately equal amount of heterospecific and conspecific pollen for each HP treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen mixtures with equal parts heterospecific and conspecific pollen

steps.description: Release the pollen from the anthers by holding each anther with a tweezer, and tapping the anther on a clean surface.
steps.searchable_summary: release pollen from anthers using tweezers

steps.description: Mix the con-and heterospecific pollen on the clean surface using tweezers or toothpicks.
steps.searchable_summary: mix con- and heterospecific pollen using tweezers or toothpicks

steps.description: Apply this mixture in saturating amounts on the stigmas of three to five flowers of the recipient plant.
steps.searchable_summary: apply pollen mixture to stigmas of recipient flowers

steps.description: Remove the anthers of recipient flowers before dehiscence to avoid self-pollination.
steps.searchable_summary: remove anthers from recipient flowers to prevent self-pollination

methods.description: This method involves collecting and counting seeds produced by treated flowers to assess the effects of HP interference.

steps.description: Collect seeds produced by each treated flower after ripening.
steps.searchable_summary: collect seeds from treated flowers post-ripening

steps.description: Count the seeds using a seed counter or by hand.
steps.searchable_summary: count seeds using a seed counter or manually
